fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer pharmacist license to delaware, pharmacist compact delaware, delaware pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Delaware Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: Division of Professional Regulation, Cannon Building, Suite 203, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Dover, DE 19904

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Delaware.

steps.title: Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Go to the DELPROS portal (delpros.delaware.gov), click 'Register', and follow the prompts to create an account and verify your email address. You will use this account to manage your application.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your DELPROS account, select 'Apply for a New License', choose 'Pharmacist' and then 'Pharmacist by Endorsement'. Fill out all required sections of the application form accurately.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload official transcripts from an ACPE-accredited pharmacy school, provide your NABP e-Profile ID, and arrange for official license verifications from all states where you currently hold or have ever held a pharmacist license (often done through NABP's Electronic Licensure Transfer Program or direct state board requests). Ensure your NAPLEX scores are accessible to the Delaware Board.

steps.title: Pass the Delaware MPJE
steps.description: Register for and successfully pass the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) specific to Delaware. Your scores will be sent to the Board directly by NABP. Note: If you have not actively practiced pharmacy for at least 1,500 hours in the past 24 months, the Board may require you to retake the NAPLEX and/or a Delaware practical examination.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the required application and reciprocity fees, currently $154, directly through the DELPROS portal using a credit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Issuance
steps.description: The Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Once approved and all requirements are met, your Delaware Pharmacist license will be issued and viewable on DELPROS and the license verification site.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Delaware. To work in Delaware, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Delaware, navigate to the DELPROS online portal, create an account, and complete the application for a new license by endorsement.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DELPROS portal, click 'Register' for a new account, complete the registration form with your personal details, and verify your email. Once registered, log in to begin the application process.
